CREATE TABLE time_intervals (id INT, interval_column INTERVAL MINUTE TO SECOND);
CREATE TABLE events (event_id INT, description VARCHAR(100), duration INTERVAL MINUTE TO SECOND);
SELECT id, interval_column, interval_column + '00:00:30'::INTERVAL MINUTE TO SECOND AS extended_interval FROM time_intervals;
SELECT e.event_id, e.description, e.duration, (e.duration + '00:10:00'::INTERVAL MINUTE TO SECOND) AS total_duration FROM events e WHERE e.duration > '00:30:00'::INTERVAL MINUTE TO SECOND;
SELECT t.id, SUM(t.interval_column) OVER (ORDER BY t.id ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS cumulative_interval FROM time_intervals t;
SELECT e.event_id, e.description, e.duration, (SELECT SUM(t.interval_column) FROM time_intervals t WHERE t.id = 1) AS adjustment_duration FROM events e;
SELECT t1.id, t1.interval_column, t2.duration FROM time_intervals t1 JOIN events t2 ON t1.id = 2 WHERE t1.interval_column < t2.duration;
CREATE TABLE nested_intervals (nested_id INT, inner_interval INTERVAL MINUTE TO SECOND);

SELECT ni.nested_id, ni.inner_interval, (SELECT SUM(t.interval_column) FROM time_intervals t WHERE t.id = 1) AS sum_interval FROM nested_intervals ni WHERE ni.inner_interval > '00:00:10'::INTERVAL MINUTE TO SECOND;
